The Londoner Recognised With Condé Nast Traveller's 2024 Readers’ Choice Award “20 in Hotels, London - Europe” » hotel magazine
evidence.fetched_text:
The Londoner Recognised With Condé Nast Traveller's 2024 Readers’ Choice Award “20 in Hotels, London - Europe” » hotel magazine

# The Londoner Recognised With Condé Nast Traveller's 2024 Readers’ Choice Award “20 in Hotels, London - Europe”

The World’s Best Hotels, Cruises, Countries, Cities, Resorts, Trains, Airlines, Airports, Spas, Islands, Beaches, Insurance, Luggage, Villas, and Tour Operators

Condé Nast Traveller announced the results of its annual Readers’ Choice Awards with The Londoner recognised as the #20 Best Hotel in London, Europe.

More than 125,000 Condé Nast Traveller readers across the United Kingdom submitted responses rating their travel experiences across the globe to offer a comprehensive look at the places they eagerly anticipate revisiting. The Readers’ Choice Awards, with their unparalleled legacy as the travel industry’s longest-running and most prestigious accolades, remain the ultimate symbol and acknowledgment of excellence within the travel sector. The full list of winners can be found here.

Patrick Katzenberg, General Manager at The Londoner, expressed: “We are thrilled to be recognised by Condé Nast Traveller’s readers as one of the top 20 hotels in the UK. This award is a testament to our team’s unwavering commitment to delivering exceptional experiences and our unique vision of contemporary luxury in the heart of London.”

The 2024 Readers’ Choice Awards are published on Condé Nast Traveller’s website at cntraveller.com/inspiration/readers-choice-awards and celebrated in the November issue.

evidence.title: An Audience With...Patrick Katzenberg | The Londoner Hotel
evidence.fetched_text:
# An Audience With...Patrick Katzenberg | The Londoner Hotel | Winged Boots

- Channel: Winged Boots | Bespoke Luxury Travel
- Length: 06:30
- Views: 69
- Likes: 1
- Published: 2025-09-22T07:06:05-07:00
- Category: Travel & Events
- Language: en

## Description

Step inside one of London’s most imaginative properties with Patrick Katzenberg, General Manager of The Londoner, as he shares the story behind the world’s first “super boutique” hotel—and what that means for the discerning traveller.

In this episode of “An Audience With…” by Winged Boots, Patrick unpacks how The Londoner merges theatrical flair with quiet luxury, combining grand scale with deeply personal service. From hidden whisky rooms and West End partnerships to immersive design details that honour the hotel’s Leicester Square roots, discover how this standout address is redefining what a city stay can feel like.

What you’ll uncover in this interview:
– Why “super boutique” is the future of urban luxury
– How The Londoner balances scale with intimacy across its 16 floors
– The art of curation—from exclusive art partnerships to whisky rarities
– What makes the hotel’s residence feel like a private members’ club
– Why Winged Boots clients choose The Londoner for bespoke city breaks

With six dining venues, two cinemas, a rooftop bar and a resident-only speakeasy, The Londoner is more than a place to stay—it’s a destination in its own right.

Discover more about bespoke city getaways with Winged Boots: www.wingedboots.co.uk

## Transcript

delighted to introduce myself and my role and the hotel and what it means to be a super boutique hotel. So my name is Patrick Kudenberg. Um I am one of the general managers here. We actually operate the property with three general managers. We as I said I look after the customer experience. We've got someone that looks and focuses on the strategy plus we've got one of our general manager focusing on the operations of the hotel. And I think maybe I explain a little bit about what this term super fatigue means to us. It's um when the chairman purchased the land here in Leicester Square um he wanted to create something and give something back to London and um super petique. The hotel is on a grand scale. We're talking 16 floors, 350 bedrooms, two cinemas, a ballroom, six food and beverage outlets, but all of the experiences here in hotel should be super boutique. So they should all be distinct, they should be all be calming, they should be exciting. um all of these things that boutique maybe stands for and what the new traveler possibly is looking for. The Londoner is quite a bold statement. Um it is quite an exciting property. We are very much connected with the theater with the West End taking the location being right on Leicester Square. So you will see knots throughout the whole hotel to the theater, the West End and all the institution that we work with. For example, you enter the lobby and you see the velvety wavy curtains in the lobby.
These are a not to the theatrical curtains. You uh you've got all of our powder rooms are actually a not to the backstage dressing rooms. You see the first outlet that you come into which is the stage, our champagne bar. It's actually served in three acts where you've got you're starting in the morning with a beautiful breakfast, afternoon tea, and then the enmo in the evening is a live performance. and our connection to the arts is not just what we've replicated in hotel but it goes back to we do have the relationships with let's say the national gallery which is right located right next to the hotel with the VNA so we do have access to some of the most soldout exhibitions some of the most sold theater shows just within the proximity of this hotel is about 40 theaters and um it's great to have these relationships with these institutions here in London and one of some of the hidden touches that you'll in the hotel is probably one of my favorites. It's the whiskey room. You'll it's hidden away. It's a speak easy bar which is part of our residence.
Basically, every guest that is staying here in hotel will have access to our residence which is the club lounge, but it's more like a private members club.
You enter into the Y bar which is designed. You've got all graffiti around you but all back projected into beautiful wood. You then go into the drawing room where guests can play board games in the afternoon. We've got a um drinks trolley there. And then we've got just next to the powder rooms, you've got a whiskey room which is just hidden away. And you've got some of the rarest bottles there. You've got a 1938 Mallen.
You've got Mommov's Kate from 1850. So, lots of different bottles. And it took over a year to create this experience of all of those whisies in there. So, it's something that's a little bit hidden and quite special to us. Yeah. How do we bring modern um experiences to life here at the hotel? I think it goes back to the super boutique again. It's very much the seamless experience, this personal touch. Um, getting to know all of the customer. That's one of the key roles that I look after. And this is for all of the team member to have these conversations with the guests and then to really bring this alive, have the conversations about guests that are in town, for the theaters, to understand what is going on in London, the exhibitions, to see it for yourself, what is going on, what's happening in London, and to really bring that back to the customer. I think to stay ahead of the curve and with the Londoner hotel scene to evolve over those years and as fast and with many openings within London we um and the expectations from the customer very much are also food and beverage lights. So I think this is one of the things that you see within the Londoner with having six different outlets. Very different from our Japanese rooftop bar to our stage to our refuel by the pool, our um wellness floor that has um a pool, a gym that is not normally what you expect in a hotel gym. It's it's a again on a grand scale.
You've got two personal trainers there.
You've got the refuel bar with where you can have some great bites either up in a cabana poolside or just after a workout.
We've you've got your hairdresser, your nail bar, you've got um a barber within the hotel. So, I think it's just meeting all of those needs that the customer might have. Plus, we've got our French Mediterranean restaurant or our pup Joshua's Tavern, which is located right next to National Gallery at the back of the hotel. Wing Boots has been an excellent partner in terms of creating and creating this seamless experience for for a customer traveling whether it is the booking experience with Wing Boots to the private jet that you've arranged to London to the arrival at the hotel where we then take over and deliver the exploitation that Wing Boots has promised the client and has um offered an experience within London.
What would 24 hours at the Londoner um include for a Wing Boot client? I think it can be everything and anything, but I think for me personally, start the day with a great breakfast in Witcoms, whether it's out on our terrace or in the main restaurant. Then go next door to the National Gallery, see some of the fantastic shows, exhibitions that are on. We've got some great ones coming up.
We just had Fangok. We've just had the um the painting the rise of Sienna, the painting exhibitions. So, I think that would be sort of my morning great lunch um either in our stage or go outside to one of the fantastic restaurants. Again, you're in the west end, you're in the heart of London. Possibly. I'd love to do a bit of sightseeing, but maybe do it differently. Take a little mini tour through London and um focus on some special subjects and then in the evenings has to be the theater. London has some incredible um productions currently and I think getting tickets to some of the soldout shows again we've got partnerships whether it's the Almeida whethe

Inside the Londoner, Leicester Square | BusinessLDN (Previously London First)

# Inside the Londoner, Leicester Square

02.12.2021

Laura Osborne and Mark Hilton spoke to Charles Oak, Hotel Director of the Londoner, a new super-boutique hotel in the heart of the capital. From opening a new 16-storey hotel in uncertain times, through to the importance of its art collection and cultural contribution, Charles shares the highs and lows of bringing this new iconic destination to the capital.

- Share this page

## [Sands China Careers 金沙中國工作機會](https://www.facebook.com/sandschinacareers?__tn__=-UC*F)
[30 August 2024](https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=920266233466575&set=a.626574472835754&__tn__=,O*F) · Shared with Public
為進一步促進澳門與内地在旅遊及商貿等多個領域的交流合作，金沙中國接待23名來自澳門旅遊大學與南京旅遊職業學院的學生及教職員，到不同的酒店營運部門及員工後勤區進行實地考察。在酒店禮賓部和前台部主管的帶領下，讓學生們深入了解酒店行業的運作流程，包括前台接待、客房管理、機械操作等。
其後澳門倫敦人酒店行政總監, Rudy Fao先生向學生們分享澳門倫敦人御園和專屬會所–華庭，如何為客戶提供的個性化專屬服務，讓學生們了解在服務行業中維持客戶關係的關鍵。
To further promote exchanges and cooperation between Macau and Mainland China in tourism and commerce, Sands China received 23 students and faculty members from UTM and Nanjing Institute of Tourism and Hospitality to have a fieldwork to different hotel …

# The Londoner Recognised With Condé Nast Traveller's 2024 Readers’ Choice Award “20 in Hotels, London - Europe”

The World’s Best Hotels, Cruises, Countries, Cities, Resorts, Trains, Airlines, Airports, Spas, Islands, Beaches, Insurance, Luggage, Villas, and Tour Operators

Condé Nast Traveller announced the results of its annual Readers’ Choice Awards with The Londoner recognised as the #20 Best Hotel in London, Europe.

More than 125,000 Condé Nast Traveller readers across the United Kingdom submitted responses rating their travel experiences across the globe to offer a comprehensive look at the places they eagerly anticipate revisiting. The Readers’ Choice Awards, with their unparalleled legacy as the travel industry’s longest-running and most prestigious accolades, remain the ultimate symbol and acknowledgment of excellence within the travel sector. The

# An Audience With...Patrick Katzenberg | The Londoner Hotel | Winged Boots

- Channel: Winged Boots | Bespoke Luxury Travel
- Length: 06:30
- Views: 69
- Likes: 1
- Published: 2025-09-22T07:06:05-07:00
- Category: Travel & Events
- Language: en

## Description

Step inside one of London’s most imaginative properties with Patrick Katzenberg, General Manager of The Londoner, as he shares the story behind the world’s first “super boutique” hotel—and what that means for the discerning traveller.

In this episode of “An Audience With…” by Winged Boots, Patrick unpacks how The Londoner merges theatrical flair with quiet luxury, combining grand scale with deeply personal service. From hidden whisky rooms and West End partnerships to immersive design details that honour the hotel’s Leicester Square roots, discover how this standout address is redefining what a city stay can feel like.

What you’ll uncover in this interview:
– Why “super boutique” is the future of urban luxury
– How The London

Inside the Londoner, Leicester Square | BusinessLDN (Previously London First)

# Inside the Londoner, Leicester Square

02.12.2021

Laura Osborne and Mark Hilton spoke to Charles Oak, Hotel Director of the Londoner, a new super-boutique hotel in the heart of the capital. From opening a new 16-storey hotel in uncertain times, through to the importance of its art collection and cultural contribution, Charles shares the highs and lows of bringing this new iconic destination to the capital.
